question.text: Using Put-Call Parity: Underlying price Rs 300, put premium Rs 4, strike Rs 250, risk-free rate 6%, 3 months to expiry. What is the call premium?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** C - P = S - K/(1+rt) = 300 - 250/(1+0.06×0.25) = 300 - 250/1.015 = 300 - 246.30 = 53.70. So C = 53.70 + 4 = Rs 57.70. **The Trap:** Rs 53.70 is C-P (not C itself). Rs 50 is S-K without discounting. Rs 4 is the put premium. Must add the put premium to S - PV(K). **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII covers put-call parity calculations. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Put-call parity is used to identify mispriced options — if actual call price deviates from 57.70, an arbitrage exists using conversion/reversal strategies.
